question.text: Theta (time decay) is typically HIGHEST (most negative) for which type of option?

answer.text: Deep ITM options with long time to expiry
ATM options with long time to expiry

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Theta is highest (most negative) for ATM options near expiry because: - ATM options have the most time value to lose - Near expiry, time decay ACCELERATES dramatically - The rate of decay is non-linear (faster as expiry approaches)
**The Trap:** While deep OTM options near expiry also decay fast, ATM options have more absolute time value to lose.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Time decay follows a "square root of time" pattern - accelerating near expiry.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maximum time decay: ATM + near expiry. Time decay is non-linear (accelerates).
